question_text: X-rays are used in hospitals to take images of bones inside the body. Why are X-rays useful for this purpose?

options.label: A
options.text: X-rays have a very long wavelength and can pass through any material

options.label: B
options.text: X-rays have a very short wavelength and high energy, allowing them to pass through soft tissue but not dense bone

options.label: C
options.text: X-rays produce heat that shows the location of bones

options.label: D
options.text: X-rays are the same as visible light but more powerful

explanation: X-rays have a very short wavelength and high energy, so they can pass through soft tissues like skin and muscle. However, they are absorbed by dense materials like bone, creating a shadow on the X-ray detector that shows the bone structure.
